user_message: Just got this from Ryan Kowalski at Wide World Importers -- I need help figuring out deployment blockers. Not urgent but would be good to have by EOD.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: deployment blockers Discussion - Wide World Importers
Date: 2024-03-25
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is deployment blockers.

[00:08:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:12:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:14:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:19:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

[00:24:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:29:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

handoffs.condition: architecture sketch needed from technical signals

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF44-G trap (Misattributes technical claim to wrong speaker (CSA vs customer engineer)); produces complete Architecture summary, blocker inventory (tech/commercial/org), open questions, sequencing recommenda
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF44-G failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF44-G failure mode (Misattributes technical claim to wrong speaker (CSA vs customer engineer))
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct deployment blockers decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF44-G -- Misattributes technical claim to wrong speaker (CSA vs customer engineer)
